title: hieracium murorum subsp. microspilon
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. microspilon, a subspecies of the hawkweed plant, is a flowering plant native to Europe and Western Asia. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its use in treating various health issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, and it is also used in topical applications such as creams and ointments to promote wound healing.
